Title: Root-knot nematodes produce functional mimics of tyrosine-sulfated plant peptides
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Synthetic sulfated MigPSY1 peptide promotes Arabidopsis root growth, supporting a functional host-development peptide mimic role that facilitates nematode parasitism rather than direct nutrient metabolite biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MigPSY1 is one of three PSY-like peptide types identified in root-knot nematodes. 2. The predicted secreted sulfated peptide stimulated root elongation in Arabidopsis tpst-1 seedlings. 3. MigPSY genes collectively act as virulence factors that support gall formation and nematode reproduction.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PSY-like sulfated peptide mimic with signal peptide
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Bioinformatic identification; synthetic sulfated peptide root growth assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous application of 100 nM synthetic tyrosine-sulfated peptide on MS medium
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome/transcript database mining from Meloidogyne databases and WormBase ParaSite; no accession specified for individual genes
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for host-induced silencing or nematode control strategies aimed at reducing gall formation and reproduction.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified only in one M. arenaria isolate; not assayed individually in nematode infection knockdown because RNAi targeted MigPSYs present in M. javanica, which lacks MigPSY1.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: M. javanica strain VW4 for infection studies
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum; Oryza sativa
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis Col-0; tpst-1 mutant; tomato Moneymaker; rice Kitaake
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MigPSY2 functions as a secreted sulfated peptide mimic that promotes root growth and contributes to early parasitism, gall formation, and successful reproduction of M. javanica on rice.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MigPSY2 was identified as a nematode PSY-like peptide gene with a secretion signal and conserved sulfation motif. 2. Synthetic sulfated MigPSY2 peptide promoted root elongation in Arabidopsis tpst-1 seedlings. 3. In M. javanica, MigPSY transcripts were highest early during infection. 4. MigPSY2 transcripts localized to subventral esophageal glands. 5. RNAi reducing MigPSY transcripts lowered gall number and egg-mass-bearing females on rice.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest early during parasitism, especially 2 days after inoculation; low by 21 days after inoculation; transcripts detected in preparasitic J2 subventral esophageal glands
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PSY-like sulfated peptide mimic with N-terminal signal peptide
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling; in situ hybridization; synthetic sulfated peptide root growth assay; siRNA knockdown followed by rice infection phenotyping
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nematode infection time course in tomato and rice; exogenous 100 nM sulfated peptide in Arabidopsis; siRNA treatment of preparasitic J2s
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Database mining from Meloidogyne resources/WormBase ParaSite; qRT-PCR expression analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising nematode virulence target for RNAi-based control or peptide-receptor interference to reduce galling and nematode fecundity in crops.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: M. javanica genome harbors one MigPSY2 copy; gene-specific biological effect in parasitism was inferred from combined MigPSY knockdown rather than single-gene silencing.

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Meloidogyne javanica; Meloidogyne incognita; Meloidogyne arenaria
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Meloidogyne javanica; Meloidogyne incognita; Meloidogyne arenaria
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: M. javanica strain VW4 for infection studies
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum; Oryza sativa
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis Col-0; tpst-1 mutant; tomato Moneymaker; rice Kitaake
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MigPSY3 is the predominant PSY-like peptide mimic in MIG nematodes; its sulfated peptide promotes Arabidopsis root growth and collective MigPSY silencing reduces rice gall formation and egg production.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MigPSY3 was the most widespread MigPSY type, with two to three genomic copies in each MIG species. 2. Synthetic sulfated MigPSY3 peptide promoted Arabidopsis root elongation, while the truncated core peptide had weaker activity than the full peptide. 3. MigPSY3 transcripts were highest early during infection. 4. In situ hybridization localized MigPSY3 transcripts to subventral esophageal glands in M. javanica and M. incognita J2s. 5. siRNA targeting MigPSYs, designed from MigPSY3 sequence, reduced galling and female egg-mass formation on rice.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest at early infection stages (2 dai), reduced at 4 and 8 dai, barely detectable by 21 dai; transcripts localized to subventral esophageal glands of preparasitic J2s
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PSY-like sulfated peptide mimic with signal peptide; conserved DY...NXXHXP motif and MigPSY3-specific two-amino-acid insertion
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Bioinformatic identification; qRT-PCR; in situ hybridization; synthetic sulfated peptide root growth assay; siRNA knockdown and rice infection assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nematode infection time course in tomato and rice; exogenous 100 nM sulfated peptide; siRNA soaking of J2s
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Database mining from Meloidogyne genome resources/WormBase ParaSite; qRT-PCR expression analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for controlling root-knot nematode parasitism because it is widespread and conserved among damaging MIG species; may be suitable for RNAi or receptor-decoy strategies.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains a unique two-amino-acid insertion in the conserved motif; truncated core peptide showed weaker root-growth activity than longer peptide, suggesting altered receptor interaction. siRNA design was based on MigPSY3 and intended to target all MigPSYs in M. javanica.
